sections.heading: Definition and Classification
sections.content: Cutaneous lymphomas are a heterogeneous group of extranodal B-cell and T-cell non-Hodgkin lymphomas (NHL) that originate in and are usually confined to the skin. They represent a broad spectrum of disorders with distinct clinical, pathological, and molecular features. The embryological origin of the skin-associated lymphoid tissue (SALT) includes lymphocytes that home to the skin.

sections.heading: Anatomical Staging Regions
sections.content: Anatomically, the skin is divided into body regions as defined by the ISCL/EORTC for staging purposes (e.g., head/neck, upper/lower arms, chest, abdomen/genital, upper/lower back/buttocks, and upper/lower legs).

sections.heading: Disease Entities
sections.content: The disease entities covered in these guidelines are: Primary Cutaneous B-Cell Lymphomas (PCMZL, PCFCL, PCDLBCL leg type); Mycosis Fungoides (MF) and Sézary Syndrome (SS); Primary Cutaneous CD30+ T-Cell Lymphoproliferative Disorders (PC-ALCL, Lymphomatoid Papulosis); Subcutaneous Panniculitis-Like T-Cell Lymphoma (SPTCL); and Primary Cutaneous Indolent T-Cell Lymphoproliferative Disorders (PCSM-TLPD, PCA-TLPD).

sections.heading: Multidisciplinary Care
sections.content: A multidisciplinary team approach is essential for diagnosis and care.
